 {"id":"bd-37f9","title":"Implement saved search storage + CLI commands","description":"## What
Implement persistence and CLI CRUD for saved searches.

## Why
Operators need a stable interface to create, list, run, and delete searches.

## How
- Add storage table + queries (create/list/get/update/delete)
- CLI: `wa search save`, `wa search saved list`, `wa search saved run`, `wa search saved delete`
- Reuse existing search execution functions; no duplicate FTS logic

## Risks
- Migration needed for new table; ensure schema migration handled.

## Success Criteria
- CLI operations round-trip correctly
- Storage layer returns deterministic ordering","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanForge","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:33.707928893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:31:06.043965270Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:30:53.514723405Z","close_reason":"implemented","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37f9","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-37f9","depends_on_id":"bd-yt9x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":27,"issue_id":"bd-37f9","author":"RubyLake","text":"Implemented CLI + storage plumbing for saved-search scheduling settings (enabled + schedule_interval_ms).

Changes:
- Storage: added update_saved_search_schedule (writer command + SQL UPDATE)
- CLI: added commands: wa search saved schedule NAME INTERVAL_MS, unschedule, enable, disable
- wa search saved list now shows schedule_ms

Validation (2026-02-06): cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test all pass.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:31:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-37zz","title":"[EPIC] Ruleset profiles + pane bookmarks","description":"## Background
Operators often want different rule packs and quick access to specific panes without complex filters.

## Goals
- Named ruleset profiles (enable/disable packs by agent/workspace)
- Pane bookmarks/aliases for quick jump and filters
- Integrate with CLI/TUI without duplicating logic

## Non-Goals
- Full workflow automation (covered elsewhere)

## Considerations
- Profiles must be safe to switch while watcher runs
- Bookmarks should not leak sensitive pane titles

## Success Criteria
- Ruleset profile switching is deterministic
- Bookmarked panes are discoverable and filterable
- Unit + e2e tests for profile switching and bookmarks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:14:10.846381056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:14:10.846381056Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-38et","title":"E2E: perf regression smoke with detailed logging","description":"## Goal
Validate performance optimizations end-to-end without regressions, with high-fidelity logs.

## Requirements
- Run a representative high-load scenario (many panes / large transcripts) using existing fixtures.
- Capture timing metrics and verbose logs as artifacts (ingest tick, pattern match, FTS query).
- Assert performance budgets are met and output remains correct.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E perf smoke passes locally and in CI.
- On failure, artifacts include full logs, timing metrics, and scenario parameters.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:32.127057017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:42.346832169Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38et","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-38et","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-38et","depends_on_id":"wa-3g9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-396b","title":"[EPIC] Pane priority + capture budgets","description":"## Background
High-volume panes can starve others and drive storage/CPU spikes. Operators need a way to prioritize critical panes.

## Goals
- Per-pane priority/weighting for capture scheduling
- Enforce capture budgets to prevent overload
- Expose diagnostics for throttling decisions

## Non-Goals
- Manual pausing of panes (covered by policy/filters)

## Considerations
- Must preserve deterministic ordering for tests
- Priorities should be configurable at runtime

## Success Criteria
- Capture scheduler honors priorities under load
- Budget limits are observable and adjustable
- Unit + e2e tests cover burst scenarios","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:04:26.257040963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:04:26.257040963Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-39wn","title":"wa robot approve: submit approval codes in robot mode","description":"# wa robot approve

## Purpose
Allow agents to submit approval codes programmatically through robot mode. This mirrors the human `wa approve` command but with JSON output suitable for automation.

## Command Interface
```bash
# Submit an approval code
wa robot approve <code>

# Submit with explicit pane context (for fingerprint validation)
wa robot approve <code> --pane 3

# Check approval status without consuming (dry-run)
wa robot approve <code> --dry-run
```

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "command": "approve",
  "data": {
    "code": "a1b2c3",
    "valid": true,
    "action_kind": "SendText",
    "pane_id": 3,
    "expires_at": 1737300000000,
    "consumed_at": 1737296400000,
    "action_fingerprint": "sha256:abc123..."
  }
}
```

## Error Cases (Stable Codes)
- E_APPROVAL_NOT_FOUND: No approval with given code
- E_APPROVAL_EXPIRED: Approval code has expired
- E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED: Approval already used
- E_FINGERPRINT_MISMATCH: Action changed since approval issued
- E_WRONG_PANE: Approval was for different pane
- E_WRONG_WORKSPACE: Approval was for different workspace

## Implementation Notes
- Query ApprovalStore for the approval token
- Validate workspace scope, expiry, and fingerprint
- Mark approval as consumed on success (unless --dry-run)
- Record in audit trail

## Relationship to Human Command
- `wa approve` (wa-nu4.3.2.12): Human-friendly CLI with colored output
- `wa robot approve` (this bead): JSON output for agent consumption
- Both use the same underlying ApprovalStore

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_approve_valid_code() {
    let store = setup_approval_store();
    let code = store.create_approval(...);
    
    let output = execute_robot(&["approve", &code]);
    assert!(output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert!(output["data"]["valid"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert!(output["data"]["consumed_at"].is_number());
}

#[test]
fn test_approve_expired_code() {
    let store = setup_approval_store();
    let code = store.create_expired_approval();
    
    let output = execute_robot(&["approve", &code]);
    assert!(!output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_APPROVAL_EXPIRED");
}

#[test]
fn test_approve_consumed_code() {
    let store = setup_approval_store();
    let code = store.create_approval(...);
    store.consume(&code);
    
    let output = execute_robot(&["approve", &code]);
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED");
}

#[test]
fn test_approve_wrong_pane() {
    let store = setup_approval_store();
    let code = store.create_approval_for_pane(5);
    
    let output = execute_robot(&["approve", &code, "--pane", "3"]);
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_WRONG_PANE");
}

#[test]
fn test_approve_dry_run_does_not_consume() {
    let store = setup_approval_store();
    let code = store.create_approval(...);
    
    execute_robot(&["approve", &code, "--dry-run"]);
    
    // Code should still be valid
    let output = execute_robot(&["approve", &code]);
    assert!(output["data"]["valid"].as_bool().unwrap());
}
```

### E2E Test
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_approval_flow.sh
set -euo pipefail
LOG="$ARTIFACT_DIR/approval_flow.log"
log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== Approval Flow E2E ==="

# 1. Try to send something that requires approval
log "Attempting send that requires approval..."
SEND_RESULT=$(wa robot send 0 "test" 2>&1 || true)
log "Send result: $SEND_RESULT"

# 2. Extract approval code from RequireApproval response
CODE=$(echo "$SEND_RESULT" | jq -r '.data.allow_once_code // empty')
if [ -z "$CODE" ]; then
    log "No approval code in response, skipping approval test"
    exit 0
fi

log "Got approval code: $CODE"

# 3. Check with dry-run first
log "Dry-run approve..."
DRY=$(wa robot approve "$CODE" --dry-run)
log "Dry-run result: $DRY"
echo "$DRY" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: dry-run"; exit 1; }

# 4. Actually approve
log "Approving..."
APPROVE=$(wa robot approve "$CODE")
log "Approve result: $APPROVE"
echo "$APPROVE" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: approve"; exit 1; }

# 5. Verify it was consumed
log "Verifying consumed..."
RETRY=$(wa robot approve "$CODE" 2>&1 || true)
log "Retry result: $RETRY"
echo "$RETRY" | jq -e '.error.code == "E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED"' || { log "FAIL: not consumed"; exit 1; }

log "=== PASS: approval_flow ==="
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Approval submission returns structured JSON
- [ ] All 6 error cases return stable error codes
- [ ] --dry-run flag works (doesn't consume)
- [ ] --pane flag for explicit context works
- [ ] Approval consumption is audited
- [ ] JSON validates against wa-robot-approve.json schema
- [ ] Unit tests pass for all error cases
- [ ] E2E test passes with detailed logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPrairie","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:51:09.929485182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:15:46.579136865Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T07:15:46.579058798Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-robot","area-safety","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39wn","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-39wn","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-39wn","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"bd-39wn","author":"DustyPrairie","text":"Implementation verified by code inspection:
- RobotApproveData struct complete (lines 896-911)
- All 6 error codes: E_APPROVAL_NOT_FOUND, E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED, E_APPROVAL_EXPIRED, E_WRONG_PANE, E_FINGERPRINT_MISMATCH, E_WRONG_WORKSPACE
- --dry-run flag (lines 1138-1149)
- --pane flag validation (lines 1112-1123)
- Audit trail recording (lines 1178-1203)
- JSON schema matches struct
- 7 unit tests found
All acceptance criteria verified.","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:15:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3gon","title":"Design WaEventSink trait and IPC protocol for native WezTerm integration","description":"## Overview

Design the interface between vendored WezTerm and wa. This defines the contract for how WezTerm will emit events to wa without Lua involvement.

## Deliverables

### 1. WaEventSink Trait Definition

\`\`\`rust
/// Trait for receiving events from WezTerm.
/// 
/// Implementations should be non-blocking and thread-safe.
/// WezTerm calls these methods from various threads.
pub trait WaEventSink: Send + Sync + 'static {
    /// Called when new output is received for a pane.
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`data\` - Raw bytes of terminal output
    fn on_pane_output(&self, pane_id: u64, data: &[u8]);
    
    /// Called when pane state changes (title, dimensions, alt-screen).
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`state\` - New state snapshot
    fn on_pane_state_change(&self, pane_id: u64, state: &WaPaneState);
    
    /// Called when a user-var (OSC 1337) is set.
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`name\` - Variable name
    /// * \`value\` - Variable value
    fn on_user_var_changed(&self, pane_id: u64, name: &str, value: &str);
    
    /// Called when a new pane is created.
    fn on_pane_created(&self, pane_id: u64, domain: &str, cwd: Option<&str>);
    
    /// Called when a pane is destroyed.
    fn on_pane_destroyed(&self, pane_id: u64);
}

/// Pane state snapshot for state change events.
pub struct WaPaneState {
    pub title: String,
    pub rows: u16,
    pub cols: u16,
    pub is_alt_screen: bool,
    pub cursor_row: u32,
    pub cursor_col: u32,
}
\`\`\`

### 2. IPC Protocol Design

Protocol for WezTerm→wa communication over Unix socket:

**Message Format**: JSON Lines (newline-delimited JSON)

\`\`\`json
{"type":"pane_output","pane_id":0,"data":"base64...","ts":1706123456789}
{"type":"state_change","pane_id":0,"state":{"title":"zsh","rows":24,"cols":80,"is_alt_screen":false},"ts":...}
{"type":"user_var","pane_id":0,"name":"wa-ready","value":"1","ts":...}
{"type":"pane_created","pane_id":1,"domain":"local","cwd":"/home/user","ts":...}
{"type":"pane_destroyed","pane_id":1,"ts":...}
\`\`\`

**Socket Location**: \`$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/wa/events.sock\` or \`/tmp/wa-$USER/events.sock\`

**Connection Model**: 
- WezTerm connects to wa socket on startup (if available)
- Reconnects with backoff if connection lost
- Events dropped silently if wa not running (non-blocking)

### 3. Configuration

WezTerm config addition:
\`\`\`lua
-- Optional: enable wa integration
config.wa_event_socket = "/tmp/wa-user/events.sock"
config.wa_event_filter = {
    pane_output = true,      -- can be high volume
    state_change = true,
    user_var = true,
    pane_lifecycle = true,
}
\`\`\`

Or via environment variable:
\`\`\`bash
export WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET="/tmp/wa/events.sock"
\`\`\`

### 4. Thread Safety Considerations

WezTerm is heavily multi-threaded:
- Terminal parsing happens on dedicated threads
- UI updates on main thread
- User-var parsing on PTY reader threads

WaEventSink must be \`Send + Sync\`:
- Use \`Arc<WaEventSink>\` for shared ownership
- Internal buffering with lock-free queues if needed
- Non-blocking sends (drop events rather than block)

### 5. Rate Limiting (Optional)

Consider rate limiting high-frequency events:
- pane_output: Could batch/throttle to reduce IPC overhead
- state_change: Already rate-limited by WezTerm's internal update frequency

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WaEventSink trait defined with doc comments
- [ ] IPC protocol documented (message types, format)
- [ ] Socket location strategy defined
- [ ] Configuration options specified
- [ ] Thread safety requirements documented
- [ ] Rate limiting strategy decided
- [ ] Design reviewed by maintainer

## Files to Create

- docs/vendored-wezterm-design.md — comprehensive design doc
- crates/wa-core/src/wezterm_native.rs — trait definitions (stub)

## References

- WezTerm mux architecture: https://github.com/wez/wezterm/tree/main/mux
- Unix socket IPC: tokio UnixStream
- JSON Lines: https://jsonlines.org/","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:41.716149348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T23:09:15.472079327Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T23:09:15.471993056Z","close_reason":"Added vendored WezTerm native event design doc + WaEventSink trait stub","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gon","depends_on_id":"bd-2xe4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.2","title":"Expose watcher health snapshot in CLI (status/health command)","description":"# Task: Expose watcher health snapshot

## Goal
Make the watcher’s health state visible so we can quickly diagnose:
- stuck tailers
- slow polling
- DB backpressure
- pattern match overload

## Metrics (example)
- discovery tick duration
- tailer count + per-domain breakdown
- per-pane last_seen + last_seq
- storage write queue depth
- event bus queue depths
- FTS insert latency

## Deliverables
- `wa status --health` (or `wa health`) prints the snapshot.
- Robot/MCP can access the same snapshot if useful.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests:
  - health snapshot JSON is schema-parseable and stable
  - “stuck pane” synthetic scenario is surfaced clearly

- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (stable JSON output in non-TTY mode)

- Diagnostic coverage:
  - `wa doctor` tests (`wa-nu4.3.4.8`) should validate that health signals are consistent with watcher state when available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- When a pane stops producing output, health snapshot highlights it clearly.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.3","title":"Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events","description":"# Task: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized)

## Goal
Generate a single artifact users can attach to bug reports that contains:
- environment info (OS, wa version)
- config summary (redacted)
- DB health stats (counts, schema version, WAL size)
- last N events + workflow step logs (redacted)
- **active pane reservations + recent reservation conflicts (redacted)**

## Safety requirements
- Never include secrets.
- Apply PolicyEngine redaction rules to any text fields.
- Prefer summaries over raw transcripts.

## Output
- A directory or archive (e.g. `.tar.zst`) written to a user-specified path.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests: `wa-nu4.3.4.7` validates:
  - redaction (no secrets)
  - stable file layout
  - stable metadata fields
  - works while watcher is running

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundle can be generated while watcher is running.
- Bundle contents are stable and documented.


LABELS: area-diagnostics, area-export, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.9.2: Implement config loader: defaults + file + env + CLI overrides; show effective config ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4: [EPIC] Diagnostics: wa doctor/status health, debug bundle, performance budgets ● P1
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.6: Retention & hygiene: prune old segments, maintenance log, explicit vacuum command ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.1: [Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.2: DB snapshot export (safe copy, WAL handling, size checks) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.5: Export audit trail (JSONL): actions, policy decisions, verification summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.6: [Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.7: Diagnostic bundle tests: redaction, file layout, stable metadata (no secrets) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.4","title":"Perf budgets + regression checks (ingest tick, pattern match, FTS inserts)","description":"# Task: Perf budgets + regression checks

## Goal
Define and enforce basic performance budgets so wa stays fast as features accrete.

## Key hot paths
- Quick reject / prefiltering (if applicable)
- Delta extraction + seq assignment
- Pattern detection over deltas
- DB writes (segments + FTS)

## Deliverables
- Criterion benches for hot paths (some may already exist; extend to cover end-to-end ingest tick).
- Budget thresholds (warn or fail) suitable for CI.

## Testing / CI expectations
- Use coarse budgets to avoid noisy failures.
- CI should detect gross regressions (order-of-magnitude) reliably and upload benchmark artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI can detect a gross regression (e.g., 10× slowdown) reliably.
- Benches are stable (avoid noisy wall-clock assertions; use coarse budgets).

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.5","title":"Metrics endpoint (Prometheus): wa watch --metrics bind + core counters/histograms","description":"# Task: Metrics endpoint (Prometheus)

## Goal
Expose optional Prometheus metrics to support long-running reliability:
- ingest lag
- gaps
- queue depths
- pattern detection latency
- workflow outcomes
- DB write latencies

## Design
- Disabled by default.
- Bind address configurable (default localhost).
- Must not significantly slow down hot path.

## Safety
- Default bind is localhost-only.
- Binding publicly requires an explicit, scary flag (proposed): `--dangerous-bind-any`.
- Metrics must never include transcript contents or secrets.

## Deliverables
- `wa watch --metrics <bind>` and/or config-driven enable.
- A minimal set of stable metric names.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - metric names are stable
  - registry can be instantiated with metrics disabled (zero overhead path)

- Integration tests:
  - start watcher with metrics enabled (bind to localhost ephemeral port)
  - fetch `/metrics` and assert:
    - response is valid Prometheus text format
    - key metrics appear (even if counts are zero)

- Safety tests:
  - default bind is localhost
  - public bind requires `--dangerous-bind-any`
  - enabling metrics does not leak secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- When enabled, `/metrics` responds with valid Prometheus text format.
- When disabled, zero overhead on hot path.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.6","title":"[Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export)","description":"# Task: `wa diag bundle`

## Goal
Provide the CLI surface described in the plan:
- `wa diag bundle --last 15m --output <path>`

This should wrap the diagnostic bundle generator (`wa-nu4.3.4.3`) and ensure the UX is great:
- clear progress logging
- clear output location
- stable exit codes

## Requirements
- Redaction always on by default.
- If requested output path exists, refuse unless `--force`.
- Include enabled features + versions in the bundle.

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.3.4.7` (bundle tests) and should include:
  - CLI exit codes and refusal behavior (`--force`)
  - stable metadata fields
  - no secrets in stdout/stderr or bundle contents

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `wa diag bundle` produces a bundle containing:
  - config summary (redacted)
  - versions
  - recent events + workflow logs
  - health snapshot
  - audit excerpts (if enabled)

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.7","title":"Diagnostic bundle tests: redaction, file layout, stable metadata (no secrets)","description":"# Task: Diagnostic bundle tests

## Goal
Ensure diagnostic bundles are reliable and safe.

## Testing
- Bundle layout contains required files.
- Redaction applied (no secrets in outputs).
- Stable metadata present (`version`, `enabled_features`).
- Reservation snapshot present when reservations exist (owner/TTL, redacted).
- If bundle already exists and `--force` not set, command fails safely.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are deterministic and do not require a running WezTerm instance (use fixture DB and fixture config).


LABELS: area-diagnostics, area-tests, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4: [EPIC] Diagnostics: wa doctor/status health, debug bundle, performance budgets ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4.3: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4.6: [Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","title":"Doctor tests: fixture-based checks + output stability (JSON/TTY)","description":"# Task: `wa doctor` tests

## Goal
Make `wa doctor` a **trustworthy** and **regression-resistant** diagnostic surface.

`wa doctor` is how users decide whether to trust the tool. If it flakes, lies, or leaks secrets, we lose users.

## Test scope

### Unit tests
- Path resolution rendering (workspace root, DB/log/lock paths) is correct and deterministic.
- JSON output shape is stable (fields present, naming stable, no secret leakage).
- Redaction is applied to any potentially sensitive field (even on error paths).

### Integration tests (fixture-driven; no real WezTerm required)
Run `wa doctor` in a hermetic test environment that simulates:
- A healthy system.
- Missing `wezterm` binary / `wezterm cli list` failure.
- Workspace not writable.
- DB unreadable / schema mismatch.
- Watcher not running / lock held / stale lock.
- Optional feature checks present/absent (mcp/browser/web/tui) without forcing those deps.

## Test strategy / harness design
- Prefer dependency injection where feasible (traits) so we can simulate:
  - `wezterm` CLI calls
  - storage open/migration version
  - watcher health snapshot
- If process-based stubbing is simpler, use a test harness that:
  - writes a small “fake wezterm” executable to a temp dir
  - prepends that temp dir to `PATH`
  - feeds deterministic stdout/stderr for specific subcommands

## Output stability requirements
- Non-TTY mode:
  - `wa doctor --format json` must be strictly machine-parseable and stable.
  - Tests validate JSON against a versioned schema (if/when we add a schema for doctor output).
- TTY mode:
  - Tests validate key content (not exact ANSI art); either strip ANSI or snapshot a normalized rendering.

## Logging & debuggability
- Tests must capture logs on failure and print them (bounded) to make failures diagnosable.
- Ensure logs do not leak secrets (redaction applied to any dynamic content).

## Deliverables
- Unit test module(s) for doctor output + redaction.
- Integration test harness utilities for stubbing wezterm/storage/watcher.
- A small set of golden fixtures covering the major failure modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa doctor` has coverage for each major check and for each major failure mode.
- Tests do not require:
  - network access
  - a real WezTerm instance
  - a real user home directory
- Adding a new doctor check requires adding at least one test covering both success and failure paths.

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure the integration harness actually uses the fake `wezterm` binary (assert invocation).
  - Add an explicit assertion that `--format json` output contains no ANSI escapes and validates against schema (once added).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayRidge","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:17:30.311960939Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:18:16.728019600Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:18:16.727950151Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-diagnostics","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.1","title":"Design: Clock abstraction + quiescence signals and contracts","description":"# Task: Design deterministic timing + quiescence

## Goal
Specify the contracts for:
- `Clock` abstraction
- wait-for utilities
- quiescence signals and semantics

## Requirements
- `Clock` contract:
  - `now()` (monotonic preferred)
  - `sleep(duration)` (or equivalent)
  - test clock can be advanced without sleeping
- Wait-for contract:
  - bounded deadline
  - exponential backoff with caps
  - structured error on timeout (include last observed values)
- Quiescence contract:
  - required signals (queue depths, last-write timestamps)
  - quiet window definition
  - interaction with "slow consumer" and backpressure

## Acceptance Criteria
- Design is specific enough to implement without ambiguity.
- Contracts make it hard to accidentally reintroduce fixed sleeps.

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.2","title":"Implement wait-for utilities (no fixed sleeps) + structured timeout errors","description":"# Task: Implement wait-for utilities

## Goal
Provide a shared wait-for library used across unit/integration/E2E tests and optionally in production control loops.

## Requirements
- APIs:
  - `wait_for(predicate, timeout, backoff)`
  - `wait_for_value(query, expected, timeout)`
  - `wait_for_quiescence(signals, timeout)` (may call into wa-upg.3.3)
- Timeout errors include:
  - what condition was expected
  - last observed state
  - retries attempted and elapsed time
- Backoff strategy is configurable but defaults to sane values.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - backoff schedule correctness
  - timeout error includes debug info

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scripts can import and use wait-for helpers.
- No new test code introduces fixed sleeps when a wait-for is appropriate.

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.3","title":"Implement quiescence detector (queue drained + quiet window)","description":"# Task: Implement quiescence detector

## Goal
Implement a deterministic quiescence detector for "the system is done".

## Requirements
- Signals (minimum viable):
  - ingest queue depth
  - storage writer queue depth
  - last segment/event timestamp (monotonic if possible)
- Quiescence definition:
  - all queues drained
  - no new segments/events for a configurable quiet window
- Provide a query API suitable for:
  - E2E harness
  - diagnostics
  - potential production "drain" operations

## Testing
- Integration tests simulate slow consumers and validate:
  - quiescence eventually achieved when producer stops
  - quiescence is not reported while queues still drain

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scripts can block on quiescence without wall-clock sleeps.
- Timeout errors are actionable (include queue depths and last timestamps).

-{"id":"wa-upg.3.4","title":"Refactor E2E scripts: remove sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence helpers","description":"# Task: Refactor E2E scripts to avoid fixed sleeps

## Goal
Systematically remove fixed sleeps from E2E scripts and replace them with deterministic wait-for/quiescence.

## Requirements
- Audit `scripts/e2e_test.sh` cases and identify any usage of:
  - `sleep`
  - implicit timing assumptions ("wait 1s")
- Replace with:
  - wait-for pane text / marker
  - wait-for watcher health signal
  - wait-for quiescence
- Improve logs to show:
  - condition waited for
  - elapsed time
  - last observed state

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scripts have no fixed sleeps except for extremely rare cases (must be justified in the script).
- Timing failures provide enough context to diagnose.

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.5","title":"Tests: wait-for/quiescence helpers (unit+integration)","description":"# Task: Tests for wait-for/quiescence helpers

## Goal
Prevent regressions in the timing primitives.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for:
  - backoff schedule behavior
  - timeout error contents
- Integration tests for:
  - quiescence with synthetic producer/consumer

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail with actionable output when timing primitives regress.

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.6","title":"E2E: flake guard mode (repeat-run a representative case)","description":"# Task: E2E flake guard mode

## Goal
Add an optional CI mode (or local mode) that reruns one representative E2E case multiple times to detect timing flakiness early.

## Requirements
- Implement a runner flag (or separate script mode) to run a chosen case N times.
- Ensure artifacts are retained on first failure with:
  - per-iteration logs
  - summary of timings

## Acceptance Criteria
- Flake guard mode can catch timing regressions without making normal CI too slow.

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.7","title":"Docs: timing determinism guidelines (no sleeps; quiescence patterns)","description":"# Task: Document timing determinism guidelines

## Goal
Prevent flakiness from re-entering the codebase by documenting best practices.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - when to use wait-for vs quiescence
  - how to set timeouts and backoff
  - how to write actionable timeout errors
  - explicit "no fixed sleep" rule + exceptions policy

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contributors can follow a short checklist to write deterministic tests.
